toolCalls.note: Total includes orchestrator (65 calls) + 14 subagent invocations with ~166 internal tool calls across all subagents. 'other' = manage_todo_list + list_dir + memory. edit_file includes replace_string_in_file, multi_replace_string_in_file across all agents.

details.reworkDetails: 3 rework cycles: (1) E2E scenario JSON edited 3 times by E2EScenarioWriter to fix grid/map config, (2) saving-throw-resolver.test.ts had 1 fix by VitestWriter, (3) plan updated from R1->R2 after SME feedback

premiumRequests.note: Both bugs (castSpell early return, null guards) caught during SME review phase before implementation. No additional premium requests needed.

extraChanges: spell-action-handler.ts modified (listed as read-only in spec, but necessary for correct implementation — same as single-agent)

notes: Multi-agent condition used 14 subagent invocations (13 successful, 1 rate-limited and retried). The orchestrator dispatched 3 SME research agents, 4 SME review agents (R1: 3 + 1 retry), 2 SME review agents (R2), 3 implementer agents, and 2 test writer agents. Plan went through 2 rounds of SME review before approval. CombatRules-SME approved R1; CombatOrchestration-SME and SpellSystem-SME required R2. The 231 total tool calls is a 3.4x increase over single-agent (68 calls). Significant overhead from: (a) each subagent re-reading files already read by other subagents, (b) plan/feedback file creation and reading, (c) multiple review rounds. The multi-agent approach did catch 2 bugs the single-agent missed: (1) missing castSpell() in total-cover early return, (2) null guards needed for map/position data. However, these were caught during review before implementation, not in code.
